title: Everyday actions
description: Tasks drawn from ordinary domestic or workplace life, treated as performance material.
context: Instructions should be drawn from ordinary everyday actions — things one might do at home, at work, or in a public space. Tidying, carrying, waiting, preparing, cleaning, arranging, looking for something. The performer treats these mundane actions as performance material, attending to them with heightened awareness. The interest is in what is revealed when ordinary activity is made deliberate and observed.
